comment_text:
This second season is way OFF from what the first season was!  I feel disappointed to the level of Game of Thrones ending. 

I've watched 3 episodes and they are just BORING. 

SPOILER ALERT: 
The 2nd episode centres around a lengthy banquet with a stage performance, and then in the 3rd episode, they make you watch yet another stage performance with an unclear aim and boring dialogue. Boring and repetitive, screentime wasted. 

I went to the internet to check WHY they've done this catastrophe with such a promising series, and it turns out they fired the first director because of artistic disagreements.

Well, you did a mistake, Netflix, you should bring that guy back! FAST! 

I can't believe my eyes with so many positive comments in the comment section. Did you guys watch the second season? It feels like watching a series from 2000.

Am I the only one feeling the same? 3 years waiting for a release for nothing.

comment_text: Oh, I wish there were more seasons. Netflix took over the series "Lucifer" from another channel and gave the fans a chance to finish it. It's a shame they didn't listen to us in this case and didn't make more seasons, probably because of the comic book creator's problems. It's a shame there's no chance we'll get more. This is the only series I still kept Netflix for. And I think that saying it's because "they only have enough material" is a lie. They wouldn't get rid of the goose that lays the golden eggs. If it was successful and had such good viewership, they would have continued it because it was simply a good business decision, and that's what it's all about at the end of the day. 😞

comment_text: Visually absolutely incredible and beautiful, but this season follows the same narrative structure as the comics, which makes it feel a bit, disjointed and unconnected. My other complain would be why they decided to change some of the most iconic moments within the comic, for example Lucifer’s last Description of God creating, a great beautiful sunset. This gives Lucifer a human side to his personality and shows empathy. Or when the Sandman decides to save the life of the demon from another who tries to destroy him. These are truly pivotal points within the story that show how the characters are not just two dimensional. I have no idea why they did this.
